title: Inconsistent signal strength between plot and json with plotImpact

posts.name: Ming-Yan Lee
posts.text:
Dear combine experts,
I notice the rounded function in the plotImpacts.py [1] script has some weird behavior if the analysis has larger uncertainties for the nominal value. I am checking the impact plots with Asimov dataset with signal strength equal to one.
I checked the input json file where the fit POI has the fitted POI
"POIs": [
{
"fit": [
-136.34979248046875,
1.0,
143.6743927001953
],
"name": "r"
}
],

However when I run the plotImpact.py script, I see the number changes to 0+/- 140.
image1292×96 5.54 KB
When I look into the combine script, I notice the nominal value changes due to the rounded option in combine. I am wondering whether this is expected and whether we should quote the rounded number or not in this case.
[1]https://github.com/cms-analysis/CombineHarvester/blob/main/CombineTools/scripts/plotImpacts.py#L28-L30
Thank you and best regards,
Ming-Yan
posts.cooked_html:
Dear combine experts,

I notice the rounded function in the plotImpacts.py [1] script has some weird behavior if the analysis has larger uncertainties for the nominal value. I am checking the impact plots with Asimov dataset with signal strength equal to one.

I checked the input json file where the fit POI has the fitted POI

"POIs": [
    {
      "fit": [
        -136.34979248046875,
        1.0,
        143.6743927001953
      ],
      "name": "r"
    }
  ],

However when I run the plotImpact.py script, I see the number changes to 0+/- 140.

image1292×96 5.54 KB

When I look into the combine script, I notice the nominal value changes due to the rounded option in combine. I am wondering whether this is expected and whether we should quote the rounded number or not in this case.

[1]https://github.com/cms-analysis/CombineHarvester/blob/main/CombineTools/scripts/plotImpacts.py#L28-L30

Thank you and best regards,
Ming-Yan

posts.name: Andrew Gilbert
posts.text:
Hi Ming-Yan,
I agree it’s not ideal. In principle these are the PDG rounding rules, but it doesn’t make sense to me in this case. I guess you can just disable it temporarily in the code. Can you make an issue in github to report this? (so we don’t forgot to look at it)
thanks,
Andrew
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Ming-Yan,

I agree it’s not ideal. In principle these are the PDG rounding rules, but it doesn’t make sense to me in this case. I guess you can just disable it temporarily in the code. Can you make an issue in github to report this? (so we don’t forgot to look at it)

thanks,
Andrew

posts.name: Ming-Yan Lee
posts.text:
Hi Andrew,
Thank you for the quick response! I am currently disable the rounding in the nominal value temporary and make a issue.
Best regards,
Ming-Yan
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Andrew,

Thank you for the quick response! I am currently disable the rounding in the nominal value temporary and make a issue.

Best regards,
Ming-Yan
